questions.question: Define erosion in the context of morphological image processing. Provide an example to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: Erosion is a morphological operation that removes small structures from an image.

questions.question: What is the difference between an opening and a closing operation in morphological image processing? Provide an example to support your answer.
questions.hint: An opening operation removes noise and small objects, while a closing operation removes large objects and connects small ones.

questions.question: Explain the concept of hit-or-miss transformation in morphological image processing. Provide a mathematical formula to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: The hit-or-miss transformation is a morphological operation that performs a logical operation on an image based on a specified structure.
